basic_info.name: Serangan (Chinese: 沙拉岸 / 海龜島; Indonesian: Pulau Serangan; nickname: Turtle Island)
basic_info.gps_location: -8.7308, 115.2375 (Pulau Serangan, southeast of Denpasar; access via causeway off Jalan Pulau Serangan; Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/SeranganSurf)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~50-70 min by motorbike (~22 km); From Uluwatu: ~40-50 min by motorbike (~20 km)

surf_conditions.wave_type: Reef break with multiple shifting A-frame peaks - both left and right peaks over a wide coral reef platform; left peak typically offers longer wave, right is shorter and tends to close out. 3-6 distinct peaks depending on swell size
surf_conditions.skill_level: Intermediate (and confident upper-beginner on smaller days at inside peaks); not suitable for true beginners due to crowded shifting peaks and sharp reef. Advanced when bigger barrel-day conditions arrive
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Waist-high to double-overhead (~2-10 ft); ideal 3-6 ft for fun rippable walls; bigger swells produce hollow barrels on the outside
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Coral reef (shallow coral platform with urchin-filled holes; live coral noted as hazard)
surf_conditions.best_season: Wet season November-March (peak December-February); the go-to east-coast spot when west coast is blown out; works year-round on small swells but cleanest in wet season
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid tide is the sweet spot (works all tides); low tide produces sucky barrels (but exposes sharp reef and urchins); high tide gives rippable walls and is safer for wipeouts
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore N to W winds (wet-season trade winds); cross-shore NW most classic; onshore E during dry season makes it choppy
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: S to SSW (works on all swell angles 1-6 ft); SSE combined with NW offshore wind is the magic combo
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (extremely consistent during wet season - a magnet that holds swell when other Bali spots are flat or onshore; the most reliable east-coast wet-season option)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 50-100 meters (rideable single-wave length up to ~100 m on the long left)
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Medium (rippable walls on higher tide); medium-fast and hollow on lower tide; outside barrel section can be fast
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate - shifting peaks make positioning tricky; drop is manageable on average days but reef is shallow and unforgiving on low tide; outside peak gets serious on bigger swells
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - 3-6 distinct shifting peaks across the wide reef: inside A-frame (mellower), middle peaks near former warung area, far-left consistent long left, outside right barrel section
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 5 (extremely crowded during wet season - one of the most crowded lineups in Bali, with hundreds of surfers and surf-school groups; cited as 9/10 crowd factor)

practical_info.access: Since 2018 most of the island was privatised for resort/mega-project development; primary access now is boat-only from Sanur or Serangan launch points - land access through the causeway is restricted by checkpoints and largely closed for surfing. Boat taxi services run daily from Sanur
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Boat drop-off directly outside the lineup; if land-accessing via causeway (when possible), walk across reef at low tide or paddle straight out through breaking waves at higher tide; return the same way
practical_info.entry_fee: Boat taxi from Sanur ~IDR 100,000-150,000 round trip per person; causeway checkpoint may charge a small ID-exchange/entry fee when land access is permitted (~IDR 5,000-15,000)
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy conditions before the school crowds arrive; mid-tide windows in early morning are ideal
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Sunrise to ~8 AM before surf-school boats arrive (crowd thins again briefly at lunchtime ~12-1 PM); late afternoon if winds stay offshore
practical_info.nearby_warung: Limited - the former beach warungs were demolished after 2018 privatisation; bring own water, snacks, wax. Warungs available back at Sanur boat launch and on Serangan village side of causeway. Mertasari Beach area in Sanur for post-surf food
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Sanur Reef (5-10 min by boat), Sindhu (10 min by boat), Hyatt Reef (5 min by boat); all on same wet-season east-coast window
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Largely yes since 2018 - boat-only access is the practical option for most surfers. Boat taxi from Sanur is the standard method

safety_culture.hazards: Shallow sharp coral reef especially at low tide, sea urchins in reef holes, crowded shifting lineup with mixed skill levels increases collision risk, occasional resort-development security restrictions, slippery dirt tracks on land approach after rain
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins (abundant in reef cracks - main concern), live coral, occasional jellyfish in wet season; rare sea snake sightings. Vinegar and tweezers for urchin spines recommended
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: Moderate to High - reef is sharp and shallow at low tide; booties recommended on low-tide sessions; not as severe as Padang/Uluwatu but still warrants caution
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Currents wash across the reef platform especially on bigger swells; channels between peaks offer paddle-out lanes; mid-tide is safest for navigating the shifting current pattern
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Despite huge crowds, vibe is generally friendly and welcoming; standard priority/no-snake rules; shifting peaks make priority claims tricky so communicate verbally. Drop-ins are common due to inexperienced school groups
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low to Medium (~5/10) - mixed crowd of locals, expats, surf-school students and visitors; no strong territorialism but lineup is chaotic rather than hostile
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Smile and say hi, do not paddle aggressively for every set, give surf-school students space, do not drop in - even though others might. Respect Balinese Hindu ceremonies at nearby Pura Sakenan temple
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: No single famous resident legend tied to Serangan; many east-coast and Sanur Indonesian surfers frequent the spot during wet season [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Pura Sakenan temple sits on Serangan Island - a significant Balinese Hindu site; respect ceremonies (especially Kuningan, Galungan, and the temple's odalan). Nyepi (Day of Silence) - no beach/sea access islandwide. The island also hosts a turtle conservation hatchery
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Siloam Hospital Denpasar (~20-30 min), BIMC Nusa Dua (~25-35 min), Sanglah General Hospital (~25 min)
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Sanur clinics (Bali Royal Hospital BROS, Kasih Ibu Sanur): ~5-8 km
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No permanent rescue jet ski; boat taxis can assist if needed [uncertain]

services.nearest_surf_school: Sanur Surf School, Chandra's Surfing Bali (Sanur), Padang Padang Surf Camp (Sanur outpost), Rip Curl School of Surf (Sanur), Bali Wave Pioneers - most run boat shuttles to Serangan in wet season
services.board_rental_availability: Available via Sanur Surf School and surf shops in Sanur; not directly on Serangan beach anymore. ~IDR 100,000-200,000/day
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - boat-based and water photographers operate during wet season; many surf schools include photo packages
services.photographer_recommendation: Chandra's Surfing Bali (~IDR 100,000 per person), Sanur Surf School photographers, Padang Padang Surf Camp daily photo/video team. Freelance shooters on Instagram (#seranganbali). Rates typically IDR 100,000-500,000/session
services.accommodation_options: Most surfers stay in Sanur (Cari Surf Camp Sesetan, Sanur Paradise Plaza, Hotel Mercure Sanur, numerous mid-range hotels and homestays); some stay in Nusa Dua or Canggu and ride in. Few stay on Serangan Island itself due to 2018 privatisation
services.webcam_url: https://www.surf-forecast.com/breaks/Serangan-Bali ; Surfline Sanur cam covers the broader region
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed for personal use over the surf zone; avoid flying over temple, turtle hatchery and any active development/resort area; check with boat captain [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Sanur has multiple coworking options: Genesis Creative Centre, Tropical Nomad (Sanur outpost), various cafe-coworks; ~5-10 min from boat launch

pricing.avg_lesson_price: USD 30-50 / IDR 450,000-800,000 group lesson (includes boat transfer in many cases); private lessons IDR 800,000-1,500,000

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Moderate - heavy wet-season runoff from Denpasar can bring trash and brown water especially after major rainstorms; cleaner in mid-day and after sustained offshore wind. Plastic pollution can be a noticeable issue on the inside

events_community.annual_competition_window: Has hosted local/regional ISC (Indonesian Surfing Championship) and Asian Surfing Championship events in wet season historically; no fixed major international event currently [uncertain]
events_community.community_events: Informal wet-season sessions; Sanur-based surf clubs often run weekend group surfs; occasional reef-clean events organised by surf schools
